formulas.name: Client-Server Model
formulas.notes: A network architecture where one computer (server) provides services to multiple clients.

formulas.name: HTTP Request/Response Cycle
formulas.notes: A process where a client sends an HTTP request to a server, which processes the request and returns a response to the client.

formulas.name: TCP/IP Model
formulas.notes: A 4-layer model used to describe the architecture of the internet, consisting of Application, Transport, Internet, and Network layers.

formulas.name: HTTP Methods
formulas.notes: A set of instructions used to interact with web resources, including GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, and others.

formulas.name: Cookie
formulas.notes: A small piece of data stored on a client's browser by a server, used to track user sessions and preferences.

formulas.name: Session
formulas.notes: A temporary storage area on a server that stores information about a user's activities and preferences during a single visit.

formulas.name: Cache
formulas.notes: A temporary storage area on a client or server that stores frequently accessed data to improve performance.
